artifact: P172 Stage 1 dynamic SLAM backend replication — 2-session cross-session evidence
claim_boundary: Stage 1 replication: 2 additional TorWIC Aisle sessions (Jun15 Run2 same-day, Jun23 Run1 cross-day) confirm trajectory-neutrality of semantic frontend masks. This upgrades the evidence from single-session to multi-session, but is still bounded to the TorWIC warehouse environment and DROID-SLAM backend.
method: 64-frame DROID-SLAM with global BA, semantic frontend masks (fork/forklift labels only), no temporal propagation, no dilation.

evidence_chain_summary.p171_single_session: 12 configs on Jun15 Run1 (64f): 7 neutral, 5 perturbed. Mask selectivity = necessary condition.
evidence_chain_summary.p172_stage1_replication: 2 additional sessions (Jun15 Run2, Jun23 Run1): both trajectory-neutral (ΔAPE ≤ 0.001 mm). Confirms frontend mask selectivity across sessions.
evidence_chain_summary.replication_status: CLEAN — no degradation across sessions for selective semantic frontend masks

claim_upgrade.strengthened: Cross-session reproducibility confirmed: semantic frontend masks are trajectory-neutral across different TorWIC Aisle sessions (same-day and cross-day)
claim_upgrade.remaining: No cross-month Aisle session tested; no Hallway scene transfer tested; ORB-SLAM3 not evaluated
